summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Officially documented environment-variable based Node.js launch override. The app sets NODE_OPTIONS to use a larger V8 old-space heap size globally, which can help large builds but also increases memory usage for Node.js processes.

setting.name: Global Node.js Memory Limit Override
setting.casual_explanation: This tweak makes Node.js start with a larger memory limit by default. It can help very large builds or monorepos, but it also makes Node processes use more memory budget.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when large JavaScript or TypeScript projects hit out-of-memory errors during builds, bundling, or type-checking.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: A larger heap can help big Node.js workloads finish successfully, but it also increases memory usage and is not necessary for most smaller projects.
setting.professional_notes: Node.js documents NODE_OPTIONS as a supported environment variable for passing CLI flags, and documents --max-old-space-size as a V8 option that sets the maximum size of the old memory section. The app writes NODE_OPTIONS = --max-old-space-size=8192 as a machine-level environment variable.

allowed_values.label: Node.js default launch behavior
allowed_values.meaning: Node.js uses its default memory behavior because no machine-level NODE_OPTIONS override is set.

allowed_values.label: 8 GiB old-space limit override
allowed_values.meaning: Node.js starts with a larger V8 old-space limit, which can help large builds but raises memory consumption.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NODE_OPTIONS = --max-old-space-size=8192 as a persistent machine-level environment variable.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Machines that have not set a machine-level NODE_OPTIONS override

states.rationale: Leave the machine-level NODE_OPTIONS override unset.

windows_defaults.notes: Without this environment variable, Node.js uses its normal runtime defaults.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Machines that frequently hit Node.js memory limits on very large builds

states.rationale: Leave Node.js on its default runtime behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You do not have consistent Node.js memory-limit issues.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Large monorepos or build pipelines regularly hit Node.js heap exhaustion.
tradeoffs: Node.js uses its stock memory behavior.
Large builds may still need a manual override if they exceed the default heap budget.

recommended_profiles.label: Large Node builds
avoid_for: Users who do not run large Node.js workloads

states.rationale: Use the documented NODE_OPTIONS mechanism to raise the old-space limit for Node.js processes.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You repeatedly hit Node.js out-of-memory problems on large builds.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the default Node.js memory footprint or run on memory-constrained systems.
tradeoffs: Large Node.js workloads get more heap headroom.
Node.js processes can consume noticeably more memory.

evidence.title: Node.js CLI documentation: NODE_OPTIONS
evidence.summary: Node.js documents NODE_OPTIONS as a supported environment variable for passing allowed CLI options before command-line arguments.

evidence.title: Node.js CLI documentation: --max-old-space-size
evidence.summary: Node.js documents --max-old-space-size as the option that sets the maximum size of V8's old memory section.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Win32_Environment class
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents persistent machine environment variables and their Windows storage model, which maps to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NODE_OPTIONS = --max-old-space-size=8192 under the machine environment registry path.

decision.why: Node.js documents NODE_OPTIONS and --max-old-space-size as supported launch controls, Microsoft documents the machine environment store, and the app writes that exact value with a straightforward rollback path.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: `NODE_OPTIONS=options...`; `--max-old-space-size=SIZE (in MiB)`; Win32_Environment: `Querying this class returns environment variables found in HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SessionManager\Environment`.
validation_proof.notes: Node.js directly documents NODE_OPTIONS and the --max-old-space-size flag. Microsoft documents that persistent machine environment variables are stored under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SessionManager\Environment. The app writes NODE_OPTIONS = --max-old-space-size=8192 to that exact machine environment store.
